questions.q: What is the President's role as head of the armed forces called?
questions.explanation: The President is Commander in Chief of the military.

questions.q: How long is a presidential term, and how many terms may a President serve?
questions.explanation: Four-year terms, limited to two by the 22nd Amendment.

questions.q: Which of these can the President do?
options: Veto bills, appoint officials (with Senate consent), and grant pardons
questions.explanation: These are core executive powers; lawmaking and taxing belong to Congress.

questions.q: Which of these can the President NOT do?
options: Make laws or raise taxes alone
questions.explanation: Only Congress can make laws and set taxes.

questions.q: Who becomes President if the President can no longer serve?
options: A new election is held immediately
questions.explanation: The Vice President is first in line of succession.
